description: Core operational system for restaurant POS. Event-sourced financial core with session management, order lifecycle, and event store. This is the operational foundation - payments, billing, and subscriptions are Phase 2+ features.

core_features.name: Health & System Status
core_features.description: Health check endpoints that verify system operational status, event store connectivity, and core engine availability.

core_features.name: Event Store Operations
core_features.description: Append-only event store that maintains immutable history. Events are the source of truth, state is a projection.
core_features.note: May require direct EventStore interface testing if no HTTP endpoints exist

core_features.name: Session Management
core_features.description: Operational session lifecycle: INACTIVE → ACTIVE → CLOSED. Sessions represent active operational context.
test_focus: Closed session cannot accept new orders
Session events are recorded in event store
core_features.note: May require direct CoreExecutor testing if no HTTP endpoints exist

core_features.name: Order Lifecycle
core_features.description: Order state machine: OPEN → LOCKED → PAID → CLOSED (or CANCELED). Orders group consumption items. Total is calculated once at LOCKED and becomes immutable.
test_focus: Order can be locked (total calculated, immutable)
Total calculation is correct and immutable after LOCKED

core_features.name: State Consistency
core_features.description: System maintains consistency through event sourcing. State can be rebuilt from events deterministically.
test_focus: State rebuilt from events matches current state

architectural_guarantees: Atomicity: No Financial Fact exists without a Legal Seal
Determinism: Replay always produces same state
Idempotency: Duplicate events are safely ignored
testing_requirements: All health endpoints must return 200 with proper status indicators
Order lifecycle must respect state machine transitions
Event store must enforce append-only and idempotency
State rebuilds must be deterministic and consistent
No direct database access - all operations via EventStore/CoreExecutor
test_environment_requirements: Test data can be seeded for orders/sessions
